conversation.content: I have been in dispute with my landlord regarding the terms of our contract, and now they are taking me to court, but I do not believe I did anything wrong, as I have always paid my rent on time and fulfilled my tenant obligations. What can I do to prove that I am not in the wrong, and what sort of evidence should I show? Are there any legal consequences on the landlord if it is proven i was wrongly accused in english law?

scratchpad:
The model should start by outlining that the landlord should have first attempted to resolve the dispute through ADR before starting the procedure of a court case.
If this has occurred with no resolution, the model should inform the user that they must gather all the evidence. This can include the contract, communications between the tenant and the landlord, and bank account statements. This would help in disproving any of the claims made by the landlord if the user believes them to be untrue.

The model should mention that if the court decides in your favour, the landlord would have to pay their/your court fees, and you could potentially sue the landlord based on the grounds of harassment. The landlord could also face criminal charges based on the serious of their conduct.

The model should answer using simple language as it can be inferred from the prompt that the user is a model.
